companies.name: Infosys Limited
companies.reason: Released Q2 FY‑25 results that beat analyst consensus by ~13% (reporting ₹3 billion EBITDA versus ₹2.7 billion expected). Announced a multi‑year strategic partnership with Microsoft to build generative‑AI solutions for tier‑1 enterprises, attracting attention from investors eyeing AI‑first services. Launched “Panache” – a cloud‑native AI‑platform for financial services, highlighted in a Delhi‑based keynote, adding product‑launch buzz.

companies.name: Tata Consultancy Services (TCS)
companies.reason: Posted FY‑25 Q2 earnings (₹12.8 bn EBITDA) upward of forecasts by roughly 7%, reflecting strong demand in digital transformation and managed services. Unveiled “TCS Inventions,” a new, open‑source AI ecosystem for rapid application development, garnering media coverage in Economic Times and Bloomberg. Signed a $5 bn contract with a U.S. financial‑services consortium to deploy AI‑driven fraud‑detection tools, boosting its product‑portfolio narrative.

companies.name: HCL Technologies Limited
companies.reason: Delivered FY‑25 Q2 earnings of ₹10.9 bn EBITDA, topping analyst expectations by approximately 4%. Announced a partnership with Google Cloud to accelerate hybrid‑cloud adoption for banking clients, announced on a New‑Delhi webinar and covered by Reuters. Closed a $200 million Series B round from SoftBank Vision Fund to scale its cybersecurity arm, creating a buzz‑worthy funding announcement in the tech‑capital circuit.
